{
  "judge_id": "demo-judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the framing issue of overstating cross-viewpoint generalization but only partially proposes a reframing by focusing on same-viewpoint results. It doesn't fully address the misleading nature of the original abstract.",
    "R1.4": "The agent mentions prior work on homography augmentation but doesn't delve into specific comparisons or contrasts with the proposed method.",
    "RX.1": "The agent correctly flags that the output lacks explicit uncertainty disclosure regarding the claims made about cross-viewpoint generalization.",
    "RX.3": "The agent accurately assesses that the output covers the requested dimensions (same-viewpoint benchmarks) without significant drift.",
    "RX.4": "The agent appropriately scores this dimension as 'no regressions' since the rewritten draft avoids introducing new errors or harmful content.",
    "RX.5": "The agent correctly identifies that the output lacks detailed provenance and traceability for the proposed changes."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the framing issue of overstating cross-viewpoint generalization but only partially proposes a reframing by focusing on same-viewpoint results. It doesn't fully address the misleading nature of the original abstract.
rationale.R1.4: The agent mentions prior work on homography augmentation but doesn't delve into specific comparisons or contrasts with the proposed method.
rationale.RX.1: The agent correctly flags that the output lacks explicit uncertainty disclosure regarding the claims made about cross-viewpoint generalization.
rationale.RX.3: The agent accurately assesses that the output covers the requested dimensions (same-viewpoint benchmarks) without significant drift.
rationale.RX.4: The agent appropriately scores this dimension as 'no regressions' since the rewritten draft avoids introducing new errors or harmful content.
rationale.RX.5: The agent correctly identifies that the output lacks detailed provenance and traceability for the proposed changes.
